payload.text:
You are an expert front-end designer and developer.

TASK  
Create a complete, production-ready personal portfolio website for a young AI entrepreneur and student, in a **single HTML file** (with embedded CSS in a <style> tag and minimal vanilla JavaScript). The page must be:

- Apple-style minimalistic, clean and “premium”
- Technological and modern (subtle animations, smooth hover effects)
- Fully responsive (mobile, tablet, desktop)
- Easy to host as a static `index.html` on Netlify
- Multilingual: Spanish (default) and English, with a simple language toggle

IMPORTANT OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS  
- Output **only** the final HTML code.  
- Do NOT wrap it in Markdown.  
- No explanations, no comments outside the HTML.  
- Use semantic HTML5 structure: `<header>`, `<main>`, `<section>`, `<footer>`, etc.

--------------------------------
BRAND & VISUAL STYLE
--------------------------------
Overall vibe:
- Minimalistic, inspired by Apple and high-end tech landing pages.
- Plenty of white space, large typography, clean layout.
- Neutral background (white or very light grey), dark text, one subtle accent color (e.g. soft blue or light purple).
- Rounded cards, slight shadows, soft borders, smooth transitions on hover.
- Use a modern sans-serif system font stack (e.g. `-apple-system, system-ui, "SF Pro Text", "Inter", sans-serif`).

Layout:
- A sticky top navigation bar with:
  - On the left: text logo (e.g. “Pablo Contreras · AI & Systems”).
  - In the middle or right: nav links that scroll smoothly to sections:
    - About
    - Projects
    - Experience
    - Skills
    - Events
    - Contact
  - On the right: compact language toggle: buttons “ES” and “EN”. ES is default and visually highlighted.

Sections (in this order):
1. Hero
2. About
3. Featured Projects
4. Experience
5. Skills
6. Events & Certifications
7. Gallery (Photos)
8. Contact / CTA

Add a smooth scroll effect for the internal navigation and subtle fade-in animations when sections enter the viewport (you can implement a simple IntersectionObserver or simple “on scroll” class toggling, but keep JS lightweight).

--------------------------------
MULTILINGUAL BEHAVIOUR (ES / EN)
--------------------------------
- The default language of the site is **Spanish (ES)**.
- Add a simple language toggle in the header: two small buttons or pills “ES” and “EN”.
- Implement a small JavaScript object that stores all texts in both languages, for example:

  const content = {
    es: { ... },
    en: { ... }
  };

- On click of each language button, update the text content of the page (headings, paragraphs, buttons, labels).
- The Spanish version should use the **original CV text** provided below (adapt it slightly for web copy if necessary).
- The English version should be a **natural, concise translation** of that content.

Do NOT use any external translation API. All translations must be included in the JS object.

--------------------------------
CONTENT TO INCLUDE (SPANISH BASE)
--------------------------------

The website is for:

Nombre: **Pablo Contreras Pérez**  
Perfil: joven emprendedor de IA, estudiante de doble grado, con proyectos reales de automatización y participación en eventos de innovación.

1) HERO (PORTADA)

- Grande, centrado, con:
  - Nombre: “Pablo Contreras Pérez”
  - Subtítulo tipo: “IA, automatización y sistemas desde Granada hacia el mundo.”
  - Una frase corta de contexto: estudiante de Ingeniería Informática + ADE (UGR), creando soluciones de IA reales para negocios.
  - Un botón primario: “Ver proyectos” (scroll a sección de proyectos).
  - Un botón secundario: “Descargar CV” (enlace a un PDF, puedes usar un placeholder como `cv/Pablo_Contreras_CV.pdf`).
  - Un pequeño texto indicando ciudad/país: “Granada, España”.

2) ABOUT (SOBRE MÍ)

Utiliza este contenido como base en español, pulido para web:

PERFIL PERSONAL (ES)
- “Estudiante de 1º de Ingeniería Informática + ADE (UGR) con mentalidad emprendedora y experiencia real creando soluciones de inteligencia artificial para agencias de marketing y automatización de negocios.”
- “Apasionado por la comunicación, la lectura y la IA inspirada en la naturaleza (especialmente Swarm Intelligence y Selección Natural).”
- “Motivado por la idea de usar la tecnología para resolver problemas reales y escalar impacto desde Granada hacia el mundo.”
- “Actualmente miembro del Programa Intensivo de Talento Emprendedor de la UGR (6 ECTS, octubre–diciembre), centrado en incubación de proyectos reales, mentorías avanzadas y validación de ideas en entornos profesionales.”
- Cita: “Todo hombre tiene dos vidas, y la segunda comienza cuando se da cuenta de que solo tiene una.” – Confucio.

Añade una pequeña sección tipo “Lo que hago ahora”:
- Estudiando 1º de Doble Grado en Ingeniería Informática y Administración de Empresas (GIIADE) en la Universidad de Granada.
- Desarrollando prototipos de IA y automatización para negocios reales.
- Conectado al ecosistema de IA y emprendimiento en Granada (UGR Emprendedora, iniciativas y eventos de IA).
- Nivel de idiomas:
  - “Inglés C1 (Cambridge).”
  - “Francés básico.”

Para la versión EN, traduce todo de forma natural.

3) FEATURED PROJECTS (PROYECTOS DESTACADOS)

Crea tarjetas (cards) limpias, con título, breve descripción, bullets y, si procede, un enlace externo.

Proyectos a incluir (ES):

a) HabitaIA – Ideas Factory UGR (2024)  
- “Proyecto de IA para agencias inmobiliarias.”  
- “Logro: cierre de 5 ventas en una hora con contratos firmados por agencias de Granada.”  
- “Diseño del flujo completo con n8n y estrategia de difusión en redes sociales.”

b) Colaboración con emprendedores de ViviAI (2025)  
- “Colaboración con emprendedores de ViviAI durante 2 meses: proyecto de inteligencia artificial para agencias inmobiliarias.”  
- “Closer de ventas: 4 contratos de cartas de intención y reuniones firmadas en 1 hora y media para Ideas Factory UGR edición 2025.”

c) Automatización para agencias de marketing  
- “Desarrollo de sistemas de captación, filtrado y contacto de leads para varias agencias de marketing.”  
- “Uso de automatización avanzada (Make.com, n8n, Google Cloud, APIs de anuncios y CRM).”

d) Motor de Animación 2D Automatizada (proyecto actual)  
- “Pipeline que convierte un texto o audio en animación 2D completa (guion → escenas → edición → export).”  

Incluye un botón o link “Ver más detalles” que lleve a `https://byte-brutalista-elegante.lovable.app/` o a su GitHub, y un card con enlace a `https://github.com/aipetrusai`.

4) EXPERIENCE (EXPERIENCIA)

Crea una sección de timeline o cards de experiencia:

- “Colaboración con agencias de marketing (2024–2025)”
  - “6 meses en CanalOnline SL, desarrollando bots de IA, automatizaciones y sistemas de apoyo para campañas de marketing (vlogs).”
  - “2 meses como colaborador en agencia freelance, creando integraciones con APIs, Notion y Make.”
  - “Formación intensiva en IA aplicada a negocio, growth y automatización.”

- “Colaboración con ViviAI (2 meses)”
  - “Proyecto de inteligencia artificial para agencias inmobiliarias.”
  - “Responsable de la parte técnica y del cierre de contratos mediante sistemas automatizados y argumentación basada en datos.”

Añade una breve sección “Cómo trabajo”:
- “Me gusta trabajar a comisión ligada a resultados, eliminando riesgo para el cliente y alineando incentivos con el impacto real de la solución.”

5) EDUCATION (FORMACIÓN ACADÉMICA)

Incluye:

- “Universidad de Granada (UGR) – 2024– …”
  - “Doble Grado en Ingeniería Informática y Administración de Empresas (GIIADE).”
  - “Nota PAU: Top 5% de la convocatoria.”

6) SKILLS (HABILIDADES TÉCNICAS)

Haz una sección con columnas o categorías:

IA y Automatización:
- “Make.com (experto).”
- “n8n (experto): agentes, webhooks, scraping, workflows complejos.”
- “ChatGPT, OpenAI, Google AI Studio.”
- “Flujos multiagente de IA, automatización empresarial (reservas, CRM, pagos, marketing, lead-gen).”

Desarrollo:
- “Python (automatización, pipelines, APIs).”
- “C++.”
- “JavaScript (incluyendo Google Apps Script).”
- “HTML/CSS.”
- “PHP.”

Cloud & Data:
- “Google Cloud: Cloud Functions, Vision, Speech, Kubernetes (nivel básico/intermedio).”
- “Firebase y Firestore (básico).”
- “Bases de datos vía API: Notion, Airtable, Sheets, GoHighLevel…”

Integración de APIs (nivel experto):
- “WhatsApp Cloud API, Twilio (voice), Meta/TikTok Ads API.”
- “Stripe / PayPal.”
- “Google Workspace (Calendar, Drive, Gmail, Tasks, Docs).”
- “CRM APIs (HubSpot, Notion, Airtable).”
- “Servicios REST con API key u OAuth.”

IA inspirada en la naturaleza:
- “Swarm Intelligence.”
- “Algoritmos evolutivos.”
- “Selección natural aplicada a optimización.”

Extra:
- “Apasionado de la mecanografía.”
- “Intereses: IA aplicada a negocios, filosofía y comunicación, biohacking y salud, creación de sistemas sostenibles, lectura y mejora personal, artes marciales, senderismo.”

7) EVENTS & CERTIFICATIONS (EVENTOS Y CERTIFICACIONES)

Crea una sección con badges o tarjetas pequeñas:

Certificaciones:
- “Google for Startups – ThePower Business School (IA y startups).”
- “Talent Land – IA & Startups.”
- “C1 de Inglés (Cambridge).”

Eventos y participación:
- “Puesto número 3 en Ideation III Andalucía Oriental (2024) – propuesta de algoritmo basado en selección natural para reducir el uso de envases en negocios, aplicando principios evolutivos a la optimización sostenible.”
- “HabitaIA – Ideas Factory UGR (2024).”
- “Nestlé Skillhaton – hackatón internacional de 3 días, desarrollando soluciones rápidas basadas en IA, prototipado y pensamiento crítico.”
- “III Congreso Andalucía de Inteligencia Artificial – asistente acreditado.”
- “Talent Land 2025 España – becado para IA y startups.”
- “DevFest 2025 Google Granada – asistencia a charlas técnicas de desarrollo e IA.”
- “Participación como ponente en actividades de UGR Emprendedora (café y networking).”

Si quieres, añade pequeños iconos tipo “trophy” para premios, “mic” para ponencias, etc. usando solo CSS (no librerías externas).

8) GALLERY (FOTOS)

Crea una sección de galería con 4–6 tarjetas con `img` y texto debajo. Usa placeholders como:

- `images/nutrition-content.jpg` – alt: “Pablo creando contenido sobre nutrición para redes sociales.”
- `images/ideas-factory-ugr.jpg` – alt: “Fotos durante Ideas Factory UGR 2025.”
- `images/dotcsv-event.jpg` – alt: “Foto con @DotCSV, divulgador de IA, en un evento.”
- `images/talent-land-malaga.jpg` – alt: “Pedro Buerbaum en Talent Land Málaga (asistencia de Pablo).”
- `images/andalucia-ai-congress.jpg` – alt: “Congreso de IA Andalucía 2025.”
- `images/devfest-granada.jpg` – alt: “DevFest 2025 Google Granada.”

You can write a short caption under each image in Spanish (and its English equivalent in the EN version). Make sure the gallery is responsive (2 columns on desktop, 1 in mobile).

9) CONTACT / CTA

At the end of the page, include:
- A short text inviting people to contact Pablo for:
  - “Proyectos de IA aplicada a negocio.”
  - “Automatización de procesos.”
  - “Colaboraciones en innovación y emprendimiento.”
- A visible email link: `pablocontreras@correo.ugr.es`.
- A link to GitHub: `https://github.com/aipetrusai`.
- A link button to his other site or portfolio: `https://byte-brutalista-elegante.lovable.app/`.

For EN version, translate the CTA naturally.

--------------------------------
TECHNICAL DETAILS
--------------------------------
- Use a single HTML file, with `<style>` in the `<head>` and `<script>` at the end of `<body>`.
- No external JS/CSS libraries (no Bootstrap, no Tailwind, etc.). A Google Font is optional, but not mandatory; system fonts are fine.
- Add smooth scrolling for navigation.
- Ensure good contrast and accessibility (proper heading structure, alt text on images, readable font sizes).
- Default language: Spanish. On clicking EN, all texts should switch to English using your JS content object.

Remember:  
**Return ONLY the final HTML code. No Markdown, no explanations.**

**Crafting a Portfolio Design**

I've been solidifying the core design concepts for Pablo's portfolio. The focus is definitely on achieving that "Apple-style minimalistic" aesthetic, ensuring a clean and premium look. Responsiveness is paramount, so I'm thinking mobile-first to guarantee a seamless experience across all devices. The single-file HTML approach seems the most efficient for Pablo's needs.


**Developing Functionality Details**

I'm now fully immersed in the technical implementation. The core structure is coming together, using HTML5 semantic elements and carefully crafting the CSS variables. My focus now is the JavaScript - the content object and language toggle are going to be key, and I'm ensuring all text will be easily translatable. I've got the animations mapped out, too: fade-in on scroll and some hover effects for the project cards.


**Planning Content Architecture**

I've been mapping out the core content and structure, starting with the header logo and navigation menu items: About, Projects, Experience, Skills, Events, and Contact. Language selection (ES/EN) is also being integrated. The hero section will highlight my name, AI focus, and student status, alongside key call-to-actions. The 'About' section will feature a profile, my current status, and a relevant quote. Project descriptions, Experience details, and Education are also starting to take form.


**Expanding Content Structure**

I've been adding content to the structure, including skills with categorized AI/Auto, Dev, Cloud, APIs, and Nature AI. I'm also planning the events section with badges for certifications. Placeholders for images with captions are being added for the gallery, and I'm ensuring all contact details (email, Github, external link) are included. The color scheme and typography, large headings and clean sans-serif are also getting firmed up. Now I need to tackle the implementation steps.


**Building Out the Code**

I'm deep into the actual implementation, with the HTML structure taking shape. The focus right now is on getting the language switching logic working seamlessly, ensuring that the default state is Spanish. Also, I'm integrating the intersection observer for those nice scroll animations. I've also done a review and refined content for the English translation to ensure readability and accuracy. Finally, I'm thinking about the mobile layout to make it look Apple style.


**Completing HTML Structure**

I'm finalizing the HTML structure now, adding the events, gallery, and contact sections, along with the footer and script tags. The `.container`, `.section`, `.card`, and `.btn` styles are getting the finishing touches, with the `.container` aiming for a max-width between 1000px and 1200px, always centered. JavaScript's essential `translations` object, `setLanguage` function, and the i18n data-attributes are all coming together.  The full HTML is the next stage, ready to bring it all to life.

Colaboración ViviAI

Formación Académica

en el preview, para que pueda ver la pagina funcionando:

**Developing the Single-File Portfolio**

I'm now focusing on implementing the single-file HTML portfolio. I've mapped out the content structure and am considering how best to integrate the bilingual support seamlessly within the one document. The design style and Spanish content are both being prioritized in my current workflow, ensuring a compelling user experience.


**Incorporating the HTML**

I'm now integrating the provided HTML content directly into the existing project. I've located the appropriate file and am working on ensuring the HTML structure aligns with the current setup. The goal is to get a functional preview immediately, so I'm focusing on basic compatibility and rendering.


**Clarifying the HTML Strategy**

I've realized the initial assumption of a React app structure conflicts with the user's specific requirement for a single HTML file. The "General code structure" description misled me. I will now directly overwrite `index.html` with the provided code. The goal is to produce a self-contained HTML document. This directly addresses the user's intent.


**Analyzing the Code Structure**

I'm now fully immersed in the given HTML code, understanding its structure. The provided document is complete with doctype, head, and body, confirming my suspicion. Updating `index.html` is the direct approach, and I've ensured compatibility with the "single HTML file" requirement by removing any conflicting script tags. The goal is to integrate the user's provided code as is, with zero interference. I will focus on rendering a fully functioning preview.


**Forming the Output**

I'm now generating the XML response, focusing on incorporating the HTML. I'm carefully referencing the HTML snippet, taking the `index.html` file into account.  I'll translate the user's intent into XML.


**Integrating the Styles**

I'm now determining how to handle the incomplete HTML snippet provided. The user explicitly wants a single HTML file but the code lacks essential `<head>` and `<style>` elements. My focus is on determining whether to incorporate the previous styling or to assume the user wants the provided body content to stand alone. The user also included the script. I'm focusing on the best way to render a complete HTML file from these pieces.


**Generating the Full HTML**

I'm now determining how to proceed with the incomplete code snippet provided. The user seems to be requesting that I integrate a body-only section into a complete HTML document, as requested in previous turns. I will reconstruct a full, functional HTML document, including the essential `<head>` and `<style>` elements, using the provided content. The goal is to fully address all instructions from previous prompts, and to incorporate the user-provided code snippet within the context of the requested HTML structure.


**Composing the Final Structure**

I'm now consolidating the portfolio elements.  I'm interpreting the "code" as the complete HTML structure, with the styles. I will generate a comprehensive `index.html` file incorporating the requested design with a focus on the Spanish translations. I will implement a complete document with header, content sections, and footer, including CSS and JavaScript to meet the design specification.


**Composing the Final Integration**

I'm now integrating the user's HTML snippet within a complete HTML structure, including CSS based on the "Apple-style minimalistic" requirement. The focus is on generating a functional `index.html` file, even though the original file list omitted it. This involves constructing the missing `<head>` and `<style>` sections, while preserving the user's script and content. I will output the complete HTML document.


**Refining the HTML Integration**

I'm now focusing on integrating the user's provided code, recognizing the omission of CSS within the snippet. The prompt's specifications ("Apple-style minimalistic," etc.) clearly indicate a requirement for CSS design.  I will generate a complete HTML document, including a `<head>` section with an embedded `<style>` element embodying the specified design. The body will contain the HTML directly provided, along with the user-provided script.


**Reconstructing the Styles**

I'm now identifying classes and their styles from the HTML snippet to reconstruct the CSS, keeping the "Apple-style minimalistic" design in mind. I need to make the `<style>` section of the HTML.  I'm examining the `index.html` file, including the usage of the script, to ensure proper CSS implementation.  I'm also removing references to any external `index.tsx` files. My goal is a self-contained, valid HTML document.


**Composing the Final Styles**

I'm now generating the CSS for the HTML document. I'm focusing on "Apple-style minimalistic" design principles, considering system fonts, background color, card styling, typography, and navbar effects. I'm prioritizing the user's provided HTML content, ensuring a cohesive and functional integration within the overall design. My goal is to produce a self-contained HTML file.


**Developing the Styles**

I'm now writing the CSS for the HTML document, prioritizing an "Apple-style minimalistic" approach. I've considered system fonts, background color, card styling, typography, and navbar effects.  I'm focusing on user-provided HTML, ensuring a cohesive design in the final HTML. My aim is a complete, self-contained HTML file.


**Constructing the Layout**

I'm now generating the HTML structure. I'm focusing on incorporating the "Apple-style minimalistic" navbar and ensuring responsiveness. The goal is to generate the HTML for the final output. The main layout will contain the header, the main content, and the footer, including the CSS design. The document needs to be a self-contained HTML file.


**Designing the Sections**

entries.description: Create complete portfolio website in a single HTML file

payload.text:
You are an expert front-end designer and developer.

GOAL  
I already have a first version of a personal portfolio site. Now I want you to **generate a new, improved version** as a single HTML file, from scratch, following these specs:

- Apple-style, minimalistic and premium.
- Technological look (smooth interactions, subtle motion).
- Fully responsive (mobile / tablet / desktop).
- Easy to deploy as a static `index.html` on Netlify.
- Multilingual: **Spanish (default)** and **English**, with a working language toggle.
- Use **Google Fonts** (Inter) via `<link>` from fonts.googleapis.com (no local download needed).

OUTPUT RULES  
- Return **only** the final HTML file.  
- No Markdown, no extra explanations, no comments outside the HTML.  
- Use semantic HTML5 (`<header>`, `<main>`, `<section>`, `<footer>`, etc.).  
- Include `<style>` in `<head>` and a small `<script>` at the end of `<body>`.

------------------------------------------------
VISUAL STYLE & LAYOUT
------------------------------------------------
General style:
- Inspired by Apple and high-end tech pages.
- Very clean, lots of white space, large modern typography.
- Background: white or very light grey.
- Text: dark grey/black.
- One accent color (soft blue or light purple) used for buttons, highlights and interactive elements.
- Rounded cards, soft shadows, smooth hover transitions.
- Font: use **Inter** via Google Fonts:
  - Include `<link rel="preconnect" ...>` and `<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">`.
  - Use `font-family: "Inter", -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif;`.

Header:
- Sticky top nav.
- Left: text logo like `Pablo Contreras · AI & Systems`.
- Center/right: nav links with smooth scroll: About, Projects, Experience, Skills, Events, Contact.
- Right: **language toggle** with two pills: `ES` and `EN`. ES is active by default (highlighted).

Sections (in order):
1. Hero
2. About
3. Featured Projects
4. Real-world Systems (pipelines / voice agents / automation)
5. Experience (timeline)
6. Education
7. Skills
8. Events & Certifications
9. Gallery (Photos)
10. Contact / CTA

------------------------------------------------
HERO SECTION (WITH INTERACTIVE BACKGROUND)
------------------------------------------------
Hero content (top of page):
- Big name: “Pablo Contreras Pérez”.
- Short subtitle, Spanish by default:  
  “IA, automatización y sistemas desde Granada hacia el mundo.”
- One sentence of context:  
  “Estudiante de Ingeniería Informática + ADE (UGR) y emprendedor de IA, creando soluciones reales para negocios.”
- Primary button: “Ver proyectos” (scroll to Projects).
- Secondary button: “Descargar CV” → link to `cv/Pablo_Contreras_CV.pdf`.
- Small text under name: “Granada, España”.

Behind the hero, implement an interactive background similar to Google Antigravity:
- Use `<canvas>` or another performant approach.
- A field of small dots/particles (in the accent color) that:
  - move slowly even when the cursor is still (gentle, fluid motion),
  - are slightly repelled by the mouse cursor, keeping a small distance,
  - keep a soft, organic animation (no crazy effects).
- The effect should be limited to the hero area only and performant on laptops.

Also add a **floating element** inspired by the Antigravity toolbar:
- A horizontal strip of circular icons or minimal shapes that drift up and down very slightly in a coordinated animation.
- Place it subtly under or next to the hero text (do not overpower the content).

------------------------------------------------
MULTILINGUAL BEHAVIOUR (ES / EN)
------------------------------------------------
- Default language: **Spanish (ES)**.
- Implement a JS `content` object with two keys: `es` and `en`.
- Each key holds all texts used in the site (headings, paragraphs, buttons, captions, etc.).
- On clicking `ES` or `EN`:
  - Update the `innerText` / `innerHTML` of all relevant elements.
  - Update the active state styling of the pills.
- Guarantee the **EN button works**: do not hard-code Spanish text directly into the HTML; instead, initialise from the content object in JS.
- Provide natural, concise English translations for all Spanish content.

------------------------------------------------
ABOUT SECTION (UPDATED CONTENT)
------------------------------------------------
Make the About section shorter and sharper:

In Spanish (base text):
- Primera frase:  
  “Estudiante de Ingeniería Informática + ADE (UGR), especializado en inteligencia artificial y automatización aplicada a negocios.”
- Después:  
  “Mentalidad emprendedora y experiencia real creando soluciones de IA para agencias de marketing, empresas de servicios y proyectos de innovación.”
- Añade al final del About, no al principio:  
  “Apasionado por la comunicación, la lectura, el alpinismo y la IA inspirada en la naturaleza (Swarm Intelligence y algoritmos evolutivos).”
- Añade una frase que mencione el inicio del camino:  
  “Empecé a emprender con IA en diciembre de 2023, aprovechando por primera vez el potencial de los modelos avanzados de lenguaje.”
- Cita de Confucio al final, en un bloque destacado.

Incluir también:
- “Actualmente miembro del Programa Intensivo de Talento Emprendedor de la UGR (6 ECTS).”
- “Miembro de la Fundación AI Research & Innovation de Granada / ecosistema de IA de Granada.”
- Idiomas (puede ir en About o Skills):
  - “Inglés C1 (Cambridge).”
  - “Francés básico.”

La versión inglesa debe ser natural y concisa, no traducción literal.

------------------------------------------------
FEATURED PROJECTS (LAYOUT CAMBIADO)
------------------------------------------------
No cards in a grid.

Layout:
- Section split into two columns on desktop:
  - Left: list of project descriptions (stacked vertically as you scroll).
  - Right: a **single image frame** that stays fixed (sticky) while scrolling inside this section.
- As the user scrolls and each project on the left becomes active, the image in the right frame changes to match that project (use different `img src` placeholders).
- When the user reaches the end of the Projects section, the image frame stops being sticky and scrolls away naturally.

Projects (Spanish content base):

1) Ideas Factory UGR – Proyecto inmobiliario con IA  
   - “Proyecto de inteligencia artificial para agencias inmobiliarias dentro de Ideas Factory UGR.”  
   - “Cierre de 5 ventas en una hora con contratos firmados por agencias de Granada.”  
   - “Responsable del diseño del flujo completo con n8n y de la estrategia de automatización.”

2) Colaboración con ViviAI (2025)  
   - “Colaboración con emprendedores de ViviAI durante 2 meses, desarrollando un sistema de IA para agencias inmobiliarias.”  
   - “Closer de ventas: 4 cartas de intención y reuniones firmadas en 1,5 horas para Ideas Factory UGR 2025.”

3) Automatización para agencias de marketing  
   - “Sistemas de captación, filtrado y contacto de leads para 4 agencias de marketing.”  
   - “Uso de automatización avanzada (Make.com, n8n, Google Cloud, APIs de anuncios y CRM).”

4) Motor de Animación 2D Automatizada (proyecto actual)  
   - “Pipeline que convierte un texto o audio en una animación 2D completa (guion → escenas → edición → export).”

Añade una frase global sobre **habilidad excepcional en ventas**:
- Ejemplo:  
  “He desarrollado una habilidad excepcional como closer de ventas para soluciones de IA, combinando datos, storytelling y demostraciones técnicas en directo.”

Cada proyecto debe tener una imagen asociada en la columna derecha con paths como:
- `images/project-ideas-factory.jpg`
- `images/project-viviai.jpg`
- `images/project-marketing-automation.jpg`
- `images/project-animation2d.jpg`

------------------------------------------------
REAL-WORLD SYSTEMS SECTION
------------------------------------------------
Crea una sección específica “Sistemas reales” o “Lo que construyo” con 3–4 bloques, usando este contenido:

1) Autocalendar de eventos y hackatones  
   - “Sistema que utiliza IA para buscar automáticamente eventos, hackatones y oportunidades relevantes.”  
   - “Me ha permitido participar en cientos de eventos sin buscar manualmente.”  

2) Agentes de Respuesta Automática por Teléfono (Voice AI Agents)  
   - “Agentes de voz con reconocimiento automático del habla, transcripción, diálogo continuo y logging.”  
   - “Envío automático de resúmenes por WhatsApp y acceso a bases de datos.”  
   - Video de ejemplo: link a  
     `https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QMcy2P98b4hXdVH3RWSex1xMn2ocemxD/view?usp=sharing`  

3) Pipeline Completo de Generación de Leads (Scraping → Filtrado → Contacto → Cierre)  
   - “Sistema automatizado para captar, filtrar y contactar leads en múltiples plataformas, con respuestas personalizadas.”  
   - “Uso de Google Cloud, APIs REST y herramientas de automatización.”  

4) Motor de animación 2D automatizada (puede referenciar al proyecto de antes).

Haz que esta sección refuerce la idea de que Pablo **implementa cosas reales en producción**, no solo experimentos.

------------------------------------------------
EXPERIENCE (TIMELINE)
------------------------------------------------
Mantén un timeline sencillo:

- 2024–2025 – Colaboración con agencias de marketing  
  - “Desarrollo de bots de IA, automatizaciones y sistemas de apoyo para campañas de marketing (vlogs).”
  - “Integraciones con APIs, Notion, Make y n8n.”

- 2025 – Colaboración con ViviAI  
  - “Proyecto de IA para agencias inmobiliarias con foco en resultados medibles y cierre de contratos.”

- Ponente y participante en ecosistema emprendedor (UGR Emprendedora y otros).

Resalta en una breve frase que:
- “Prefiero trabajar con modelos de comisión o variables ligadas a resultados, eliminando riesgo para el cliente y alineando incentivos.”

------------------------------------------------
EDUCATION
------------------------------------------------
Sección clara, tipo card:

- “Universidad de Granada (UGR), 2024–presente”  
  - “Doble Grado en Ingeniería Informática y Administración de Empresas (GIIADE).”  
  - “Nota PAU: Top 5 % de la convocatoria.”

------------------------------------------------
SKILLS (ACTUALIZADO)
------------------------------------------------
Organiza por categorías, mostrando nivel visual (por ejemplo, etiquetas o pequeñas barras):

IA y Automatización:
- Make.com (experto).
- n8n (experto): agentes, webhooks, scraping, workflows complejos.
- ChatGPT / OpenAI (usando modelos 3, 4, 5 desde 2023).
- Google AI Studio (Gemini, modelos de vídeo).
- Sistemas multiagente de IA.
- Automatización empresarial: reservas, CRM, pagos, campañas de marketing, lead-gen.

Lenguajes & Desarrollo:
- Python (automatización, pipelines, APIs).
- R (data analysis).
- JavaScript (incluyendo Google Apps Script).
- C++.
- HTML/CSS.
- PHP.
- Webhooks, OAuth, autenticación y manejo de APIs REST.

Cloud & Data:
- Google Cloud: Cloud Functions, Vision, Speech, Kubernetes (nivel básico/intermedio).
- Firebase y Firestore (básico).
- Bases de datos vía API: Notion, Airtable, Sheets, GoHighLevel…
- Conocimientos iniciales de AWS (mención breve).

Herramientas e IDE:
- Google Antigravity IDE.
- VS Code.
- GitHub.

APIs (nivel experto):
- WhatsApp Cloud API, Twilio (voice).
- Meta/TikTok Ads API.
- Stripe / PayPal.
- Google Workspace (Calendar, Drive, Gmail, Tasks, Docs).
- CRM APIs (HubSpot, Notion, Airtable).
- APIs de imagen: Unsplash, vision / photo models.
- Cualquier servicio REST con API key u OAuth.

Extra:
- Apasionado de la mecanografía.
- Intereses: IA aplicada a negocios, filosofía y comunicación, biohacking y salud, sistemas sostenibles, lectura, artes marciales, senderismo.

------------------------------------------------
EVENTS & CERTIFICATIONS (WITH LINKS)
------------------------------------------------
Cada ítem debe ser clicable (enlace a certificado o recurso externo). Usa botones o tags estilizados.

Certificaciones:
- “Google for Startups – ThePower Business School (IA y startups).”
- “Talent Land – IA & Startups.”
- “C1 de Inglés (Cambridge).”

Eventos y participación (ejemplos de links, usa `href` reales o placeholders):
- “Top 3 en Ideation III Andalucía Oriental (2024)” → link placeholder (ej. Notion o PDF).
- “Ideas Factory UGR – Proyecto de IA inmobiliaria” → link a `https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qTv5DnqwSUAmEflrkUe7Hw6YkXDT9OPlB-CPsQKtMYA/edit`.
- “Nestlé Skillhaton – hackatón internacional de 3 días” → link a  
  `https://drive.google.com/file/d/1YFLECR2FQBrBAEVNUOnIODhBZ_R7Chvn/view?usp=drive_link`
- “III Congreso Andalucía de Inteligencia Artificial – asistencia acreditada.”
- “Talent Land 2025 España – becado para IA y startups.”
- “DevFest 2025 Google Granada – asistencia a charlas técnicas de desarrollo e IA.”
- “Ponente en actividades de UGR Emprendedora (café y networking).”

------------------------------------------------
GALLERY
------------------------------------------------
Responsive gallery (2 columns desktop, 1 on mobile) with images and captions:

Use image placeholders like:
- `images/nutrition-content.jpg` – caption ES: “Creando contenido sobre nutrición y rendimiento.”  
- `images/ideas-factory-ugr.jpg` – “Ideas Factory UGR 2025.”  
- `images/voice-agent.jpg` – “Prototipo de agente de voz con IA en producción.”  
- `images/chatgpt-subscription.jpg` – “Más de un año iterando con modelos avanzados de IA.”  
- `images/api-flow.jpg` – “Primeros flujos de automatización y uso de la API de OpenAI.”  
- `images/ai-events.jpg` – “Eventos de IA y comunidad en Granada.”

Añade traducciones equivalentes en EN.

------------------------------------------------
CONTACT / CTA
------------------------------------------------
At the end:

Spanish:
- “¿Hablamos?”  
- “Si quieres llevar la IA a tu negocio, automatizar procesos o explorar nuevas soluciones, escríbeme.”
- Email visible y clicable: `pablocontreras@correo.ugr.es`.
- Botón/link a GitHub: `https://github.com/aipetrusai`.
- Botón/link a otra web o portfolio: `https://byte-brutalista-elegante.lovable.app/`.

English version: natural translation (“Let’s talk”, etc.).

------------------------------------------------
FOOTER
------------------------------------------------
Simple, centered footer with small text:

- “Built by Petrus.”  
- No references to online editors or generic builders.

------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL DETAILS SUMMARY
------------------------------------------------
- Single HTML file (`index.html`) with `<style>` in `<head>` and `<script>` at end of `<body>`.
- No external JS/CSS libraries (no Bootstrap, no Tailwind, etc.).
- You MAY use Google Fonts (Inter) via `<link>`.
- Smooth scrolling for internal navigation.
- Working language toggle (ES/EN) using a JS content object.
- Interactive hero background (particle field) + subtle floating icons strip.
- Accessible: good color contrast, alt text on images, proper heading hierarchy, readable fonts.

Remember:  
**Return ONLY the final HTML code. No Markdown, no explanations.**


FONT REQUIREMENTS (VERY IMPORTANT)

At the top of the <style> block, add exactly this:

@font-face {
  font-family: "BrandFont";
  src: url("/fonts/brand-font.woff2") format("woff2");
  font-weight: 400;
  font-style: normal;
  font-display: swap;
}

Set the global font so that ALL text in the website uses this font:

:root {
  font-family: "BrandFont", -apple-system, system-ui, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", sans-serif;
}

body {
  margin: 0;
  font-family: inherit;
}

* {
  font-family: inherit;
}

Do not declare any other font-family anywhere in the CSS. 
All components, headings, buttons and text must inherit from :root.

**Developing the Design**

I'm solidifying the website's structure. The Apple-style minimalism is taking shape: clean lines, ample white space, and a focus on visual hierarchy. I'm prioritizing responsiveness across devices. Bilingual support is the next major hurdle, ensuring a seamless experience for Spanish and English speakers. Deployment on Netlify is firmly in my sights.


**Implementing the Structure**

The single HTML file structure is now concrete, with the sections: Hero, About, Featured Projects, Real-world Systems, Experience, Education, Skills, Events, Gallery, and Contact. Crucially, I've incorporated the `<style>` and `<script>` tags for embedded CSS and JS. Currently integrating the custom `@font-face` for "BrandFont" is top priority. Implementing the interactive background in the Hero section, and the sticky header/language toggle are up next.


**Structuring the Sections**

I've fleshed out the section content with details for "About," "Featured Projects," and "Real-world Systems."  Experience and Education are defined, along with skills categorized by IA, Dev, Cloud, Tools, and APIs. A curated Events list is ready, plus a Gallery grid. Finally, contact info is set. I'm focusing on HTML structuring now, using semantic tags, and I've detailed my CSS approach.


**Constructing the Interaction**

I'm now fully immersed in the core interactivity. The language toggle is working seamlessly, using an object-driven string switch. I've got a mesmerizing hero canvas animation with repelling dots, driven by the mouse. Intersection Observer is now powering smooth scroll animations, revealing content as you explore.  I've devised the logic for those sticky "Featured Projects" images, too, updating based on scroll position. Smooth scrolling to anchors is also implemented.

**Coding the Visuals**

The hero canvas is up and running: I've created the `<canvas>` element and 2D context. The particle array and mouse event listener are integrated, and the animation loop is driving those smooth particle movements.  The custom CSS rules are dialed in to make it pretty.

**Refining the Project Switcher**

I am now able to switch the source of the image by checking if the project text block is visible. The project details are updated in real-time, matching the visible content.


**Integrating Font Choices**

I've resolved the font ambiguity by prioritizing "BrandFont" locally, and implementing the Google Font "Inter" as backup, as instructed.  This approach satisfies all requirements: "BrandFont" gets the first preference, with Inter as a fallback. I've successfully implemented both the font link tag and the custom `@font-face` rule. This approach also allows for future flexibility should the `/fonts/brand-font.woff2` ever be available.


**Addressing Font Flexibility**

I've taken a conservative approach to the font strategy. To ensure maximum compatibility and flexibility, I'm setting "Inter" as the second fallback in the `:root` definition, even though the current CSS doesn't use it directly. This guarantees visual consistency should "BrandFont" be unavailable. I've also incorporated a `<link>` to the Google Fonts CDN for "Inter" in the `<head>` of the HTML, making sure Inter will be fetched in advance.


**Orchestrating the Visuals**

I'm now building the "Floating field of small dots/particles" canvas animation with a slightly repelling mouse effect. I'm focusing on gentle, fluid movement.  The Real-world Systems section is taking shape with a grid layout. My language object is ready, waiting for the Spanish translations. I'm preparing to implement an IntersectionObserver to drive the image swap.

entries.description: Create a complete, single-file HTML portfolio for Pablo Contreras Pérez with the requested Apple-style design, interactive hero background, sticky project images, multilingual support, and specific content updates.
